diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support. How can I help you today?
[Caller] Hi, quick question. How do I look up my UID inside the game?
[Agent] Happy to walk you through it. Your UID is a nine-digit number shown in the bottom-right corner of the in-game screen, on every menu and the title screen. The first digit tells you your server region as well — 6 for America, 7 for Europe, 8 for Asia, and 9 for TW HK MO.
[Caller] Oh, that's handy. And what does the first digit of mine tell me if it starts with 7?
[Agent] A UID starting with 7 means your account is on the Europe server.
[Caller] Perfect. One more thing — can I move my UID to a different region if I want to play with friends in Asia?
[Agent] Unfortunately, cross-region transfers aren't supported. A UID stays permanently on the region it was created on. You'd need to start a separate account on the Asia server to play with your friends there, but your purchases and progress wouldn't carry over.
[Caller] Got it, thanks for the clear answer.
[Agent] You're welcome! Is there anything else I can help with?
[Caller] No, that's all.
[Agent] Have a great day, and safe travels in Teyvat!
